notes: postpositive — never appears first in clause
οὐ before consonants; οὐκ before smooth breathing; οὐχ before rough breathing
takes accusative (usually); "with" in sense of "in the presence of"
ὁ θεός; can mean "a god" without article
ὁ ἀδελφός; plural can mean "brothers and sisters"
ὁ οὐρανός; often plural οἱ οὐρανοί
irregular declension: gen/dat Ἰησοῦ, acc Ἰησοῦν
regular -ω verb; takes genitive or accusative object
declines like 2-1-2; substantive use "saint, holy one"
